steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage describes three sequential steps: (1) scraping the ice, (2) spraying water, (3) smoothing the ice. The blank introduces step 2. The correct transition must signal that this is the next step in an ordered sequence, not a contrast, example, or comparison.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — For example,: Signals that what follows is an illustration of what came before. Step 2 is not an example of step 1 — it is a separate action in a sequence. ✗
  • Choice B — Next,: Signals the next step in a sequence. The passage follows a strict first-then-lastly structure, and "Next" fits perfectly between "first" (step 1) and "Lastly" (step 3). ✓
  • Choice C — Similarly,: Signals that what follows resembles what came before. Spraying water and scraping ice are not parallel or similar actions. ✗
  • Choice D — In contrast,: Signals opposition or reversal. Step 2 does not contradict step 1 — the steps build on each other. ✗
That is the correct answer.
